figures.caption: Fig. 3. Success, failure, and composite metrics for each method on the (a) LIBERO-Spatial, (b) Square, and (c) Cloth Folding tasks (dataset size 200 for the simulation experiments and 150 for the hardware experiment, 5 training seeds, higher is better; error bars denote standard deviation).
TABLE III CLOTH FOLDING TASK HYPERPARAMETERS
Fig. 4. Our method’s predicted values over a successful Square task episode showing a false-positive high value due to a poor grasp that managed to succeed.

Fig. 5. Dataset size ablation showing success metric vs. failure metric (higher is better for both) for each method on the (a) LIBERO-Spatial, (b) Square, and (c) Cloth Folding tasks across 5 training seeds. Colors distinguish methods while shapes distinguish dataset sizes; error bars denote standard deviation. Cloth Folding is evaluated only up to dataset size 150.

Fig. 2. Visualization of the bootstrap mechanism introduced in Sec. V-C. Episode 1 contains 2 goal states, and all states in this episode have welldefined values (i.e., V π(sk1) < 1 ∀ 0 < k ≤ 6). Episode 2 is terminated by time-out, but one of its states s32 intersects with Episode 1. Lastly, Episode 3 is terminated by time-out and does not intersect with any episodes with goal states. In this case, all of its states take on the default sparse reward value of 1.
